mapping.killer_term: Midnight anxiety sessions / Staring at the ceiling at 3 AM
Brain stuck in survival mode / Mental bandwidth depleted
Your financial house is on fire
Closing the app in horror
Cardboard generic pasta / Crappy airport sandwiches
Searching couch cushions for gas money

phrase_patterns.boring_phrase: You are worried about your bills.
phrase_patterns.killer_phrase: You're staring at the ceiling at 3 AM, wondering where it all went wrong.

phrase_patterns.boring_phrase: Being broke makes it hard to think.
phrase_patterns.killer_phrase: Your brain is so consumed by survival-based calculations that you have zero bandwidth for growth.

mapping.killer_term: Automotive indentured servitude / Financial hamster wheel
Gleaming monster with cup holders / Chrome everything
Automotive Stockholm Syndrome
Active wealth destruction / Liquidating your future
Performing mental gymnastics to justify it
A financial horror movie disguised as a promotion

phrase_patterns.boring_phrase: Car debt is a bad idea.
phrase_patterns.killer_phrase: You're defending that truck payment like it's a family member while it's actively destroying your wealth.

phrase_patterns.boring_phrase: Don't buy things to look rich.
phrase_patterns.killer_phrase: You've signed up for a life on the financial hamster wheel just to look rich to people you don't even like.

mapping.killer_term: Aggressive accumulation
Gravity / Comical acceleration
Pushing the boulder / Cresting the hill
Money doing the heavy lifting / Automatic momentum

phrase_patterns.boring_phrase: Saving is hard at the start.
phrase_patterns.killer_phrase: The first $20,000 is a battle against gravity; you're pushing a heavy boulder up a steep hill.
Once you crest the hill, gravity starts working for you. The acceleration becomes almost comical.

mapping.killer_term: Financial oxygen / Breathing room / Safety net permission
Walking away money / Secret superpower
Assertive demeanor / Negotiating from strength
Eliminating decision fatigue / Signal over noise

phrase_patterns.killer_phrase: With $20k in the bank, you're not just a worker; you're a man negotiating from a position of absolute strength.

phrase_patterns.boring_phrase: You don't have to stay at a bad job.
phrase_patterns.killer_phrase: You have 'Walking Away Money'. It's the superpower that makes you immune to toxic workplace dynamics.

mapping.killer_term: Fly out and knock on the door / Deciphering the mechanics
Undeniable data / Hard evidence / No sugar-coating
Brutal reality check / Not here to hold your hand

phrase_patterns.boring_phrase: I checked if he was real.
phrase_patterns.killer_phrase: I was skeptical, so I decided to fly out, knock on his door, and see the numbers for myself.

phrase_patterns.boring_phrase: The numbers prove I am right.
phrase_patterns.killer_phrase: The numbers don't lie. Vanguard research proves this single shift increases your happiness by 21%.

structural_rules: 90/10 Ratio: 90% simple English, 10% Killer Power Phrases.
Visual Anchoring: Every abstract concept (debt, growth) must use a physical metaphor (hamster wheel, boulder).
No Hedges: Remove 'I think', 'maybe', 'possibly'. Use absolute clinical statements.
